message:
chore(beads): update issue tracking state for obligation, fixture, and profile beads

Update .beads/issues.jsonl to reflect current bead lifecycle state:
- Close completed beads (bd-296.8 traceability index, bd-1md.13 core
  fixture families, bd-1md.14 robustness fixtures, bd-1md.15 vertical
  continuity fixtures, among others)
- Update dependency metadata and close-reason annotations
- Sync bead status with delivered documentation and schema artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-296.8","title":"Create plan-to-execution traceability index (claims -> beads -> tests -> artifacts)","description":"Background:
Future maintainers should not need to re-read the giant plan to understand why work exists.

Subtasks:
- Build section-by-section index mapping plan sections 0 through 21 to bead IDs, acceptance artifacts, and owning milestones.
- Include mapping from quality gates to concrete CI jobs/artifacts and failure triage commands.
- Include mapping from profile promises to fixture families, digest checks, and metric budgets.
- Include mapping for open decisions, deferred surfaces, and risk controls so governance context is self-contained.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Every major plan section has at least one mapped bead and acceptance artifact.
- Index is self-contained and update protocol is documented.
- Enables audit/review without archaeology and without reopening the plan  {"id":"bd-2cw","title":"EPIC: Runtime Kernel, Scheduling, Cancellation, Timer, and Channel Semantics","description":"Background:
Kernel behavior is where semantic fidelity is most fragile: ordering, cancellation, and queueing rules can silently diverge.

Goal:
Implement deterministic kernel loop and channel/timer semantics with rigorous fairness/starvation and correctness checks.

Must include:
- deterministic tie-break and event sequencing
- cancellation propagation checkpoints
- timer wheel semantics and O(1) cancel behavior
- bounded two-phase MPSC semantics

Why this serves users:
- reproducible behavior for incident analysis
- robust performance and correctness on both low-latency and constrained hardware.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.106645851Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:20:12.160584077Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-5","phase-6","runtime","scheduler"]}

 {"id":"bd-2cw.2","title":"Implement deterministic scheduler loop with stable tie-break ordering and event sequencing","description":"Background:
Scheduler ordering drift is a primary semantic regression vector and must be tightly controlled.

Subtasks:
- Implement deterministic ready/cancel/timed lane processing rules.
- Define stable tie-break ordering for simultaneous events.
- Emit deterministic sequencing metadata suitable for replay identity.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Same input and seed produce identical ordered event streams.
- Ordering rules are documented and fixture-tested.
- No hidden nondeterminism from container iteration order.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.342327824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:35.523369962Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","phase-5","runtime","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.342327824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.864055560Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.519795292Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:40.164646182Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.944585878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.3","title":"Implement cancellation propagation, checkpoints, and bounded cleanup semantics","description":"Background:
Cancellation is central to asupersync semantics and must preserve bounded cleanup with explicit phase behavior.

Subtasks:
- Implement cancellation propagation through region/task relationships.
- Implement task-visible checkpoint API and phase transitions.
- Verify cleanup boundaries and obligations under cancel storms.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Cancel behavior matches fixture expectations for edge ordering cases.
- Cleanup remains bounded and deterministic.
- All cancel-phase transitions are legality-checked.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.423775291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:35.390543320Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cancellation","phase-5","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.423775291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:32.023801673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:57:39.599269615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.4","title":"Implement timer wheel semantics with deterministic ordering and O(1) cancel by validated handle","description":"Background:
Timer behavior affects fairness, deadline semantics, and replay fidelity.

Subtasks:
- Implement timer insertion/fire/cancel semantics with explicit ordering rules.
- Enforce generation-validated cancel handles.
- Add boundary tests for churn and cancellation races.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Timer ordering is deterministic for same inputs.
- Cancel operations are O(1) by design and measured.
- Failure behavior on invalid handles is stable and explicit.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.503960933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:35.261472584Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-5","runtime","timer"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.503960933Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:32.104467334Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:30.965570531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.5","title":"Build small-state exhaustive scheduler checker and cycle-budget checkpoint validation","description":"Background:
Random tests miss starvation and fairness corner cases; small-state exhaustive analysis is required.

Subtasks:
- Build bounded state-space explorer for scheduler transitions.
- Validate fairness/starvation properties against expected invariants.
- Validate cycle-budget checkpoint behavior under low-frequency CPU assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Checker catches seeded adversarial ordering bugs.
- Output includes minimized counterexample traces for failures.
- Results are reproducible and CI-friendly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.594112372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:40.011161977Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["model-check","phase-5","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.594112372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:32.187439358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T01:01:31.057174491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:32.265302508Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cw.6","title":"Implement bounded MPSC two-phase channel (reserve/send/abort) with FIFO and wakeup correctness","description":"Background:
Two-phase channel semantics are key to obligation linearity and correctness under backpressure.

Subtasks:
- Implement reserve/send/abort transitions and legality checks.
- Enforce bounded capacity behavior and deterministic rejection/backpressure semantics.
- Validate FIFO and wakeup behavior under concurrent producer patterns.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Channel conformance fixtures pass for success/failure paths.
- No illegal state transitions survive API boundaries.
- Detailed logs exist for deadlock/starvation debugging in tests.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.682771083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:35.129907074Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["channel","phase-6","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:31.682771083Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2cw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:32.343680222Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2cw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-hwb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:49:32.420429063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Subtasks:
- Implement worker model and lane scheduling rules for ready/cancel/timed work.
- Implement bounded fairness controls and test harnesses.
- Validate no semantic regression in deterministic single-thread profile.

 {"id":"bd-2n0","title":"EPIC: Dual Codec (JSON/BIN), Trace, Replay, and Continuity","description":"Background:
JSON-first comparison and optimized binary transport must coexist without semantic divergence.

Goal:
Deliver codec abstraction, trace/replay pipeline, and crash/restart continuity with canonical semantic digests.

Guarantee:
Codec choice may affect throughput/footprint only; it may not alter semantic outcomes.

Why this serves users:
- JSON enables inspectability and easier parity debugging
- binary enables production efficiency
- replay continuity supports post-failure forensics.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.176136721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:40.538156697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","phase-7","replay"]}

 {"id":"bd-2n0.2","title":"Implement optimized binary codec (length-delimited, compact fields, checksum, safe zero-copy paths)","description":"Background:
Binary mode is needed for throughput/latency/footprint gains while preserving exact semantics.

Subtasks:
- Implement frame format with message type and schema versioning.
- Implement compact field encoding and optional integrity footer/checksum.
- Implement safe zero-copy decode for hot payloads where invariants permit.

 {"id":"bd-2n0.4","title":"Implement deterministic event emission format, replay mode, and snapshot/export hooks","description":"Background:
Replayability is a cornerstone guarantee for debugging, auditing, and regression analysis.

Subtasks:
- Implement deterministic event record format and hash-chain output.
- Implement replay input mode with verification of expected state/snapshot.

 {"id":"bd-hwb","title":"EPIC: Core Semantics and Guarantee Substitution in ANSI C/C99","description":"Background:
Rust compile-time guarantees are absent in C. We must reconstitute equivalent runtime/proof guarantees via explicit mechanisms.

Goal:
Implement asx_core semantic primitives and safety scaffolding with deterministic failure behavior.

Core obligations:
- explicit state authorities
- generation-safe handles and ownership boundaries
- ghost monitors and linearity checks
- strict resource exhaustion semantics
- canonical error taxonomy and stability policy

Why this serves users:
- correctness under pressure for embedded systems
- predictable failure semantics for high-stakes systems
- confidence that performance tuning does not erase guarantees.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.038789705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:19:04.939650111Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","phase-3","phase-4","safety","semantics"]}

 {"id":"bd-j4m","title":"EPIC: Cross-Vertical Profiles (Embedded Router, HFT, Automotive) with Zero Semantic Drift","description":"Background:
This project must serve multiple hard domains without splitting semantics into incompatible forks.

Goal:
Implement profile-specific operational tuning while enforcing semantic digest equivalence across shared fixtures.

Includes:
- embedded resource classes (R1/R2/R3)
- HFT tail/jitter governance
- automotive deadline/watchdog/degraded-mode behavior
- vertical adapters with fallback isomorphism artifacts

Why this serves users:
- domain-specific utility without losing the core architecture
- confidence that profile tuning does not compromise correctness.

Cross-cutting quality contract:
- Child tasks must define and link unit tests, e2e script coverage (when behavioral), and structured log/manifest artifacts or explicit rationale for non-applicability.
- Completion evidence must remain compatible with bd-66l.9 enforcement and traceability index expectations.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-27T00:47:42.317289058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T02:11:37.496783856Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automotive","embedded","hft","phase-8","phase-9","profiles"]}
